content: My ultimate goal is to build amazing projects on my GitHub that get a ton of stars.
Understood! I will act as Orion and manage the startup factory.

Today we migrated the R&D swarm to push to a single sayedRaheel/startup-factory monorepo. I fixed the formatting bugs (forcing ### instead of **) and updated the root README.md to feature a clean Markdown table with auto-extracted short descriptions of the daily builds.

Today we completely extracted Sayed's RLM-Memory paper into a standalone python package called 'Clawmory RLM', and started building a hybrid RLM+RAG proxy that gives OpenClaw infinite, flawless local memory. We just added local Ollama support (Qwen 2.5 3B) so the sub-agent swarm costs /bin/zsh to run.
Modified the rnd-lab swarm script (run-daily-enhanced.sh) to publish daily AI-generated startups to a clean monorepo (sayedRaheel/startup-factory) instead of generating 365 individual repos per year.
Updated the swarm script to force the AI to use proper Markdown sub-headers (###) in README.md instead of bold text (**).
Updated the swarm script to extract short descriptions from generated projects and automatically format the root Monorepo README.md into a clean Markdown table.
Cleaned up duplicate daily test runs from the startup-factory monorepo.
Confirmed the 9:00 AM daily cron job is correctly configured for isolated, background startup generation.
We successfully updated the local Ollama installation and downloaded the cutting-edge qwen3.5:4b model to power the RLM memory sub-agents for free.
Before we build the proxy, I want to rigorously test the RLM engine to ensure it never hallucinates.
